command:
echo "=== resolv.conf BEFORE test ==="
md5sum /etc/resolv.conf
cat /etc/resolv.conf

echo ""
echo "=== Simulate the reset trigger: run dhcpcd rebind (the thing that rewrites it) ==="
# dhcpcd -n rebinds without dropping the interface; with nohook it should NOT touch resolv.conf
sudo dhcpcd --rebind ens33 2>&1 | head -5 || echo "rebind returned (expected if not leasing)"
sleep 3

echo ""
echo "=== resolv.conf AFTER dhcpcd rebind ==="
md5sum /etc/resolv.conf
cat /etc/resolv.conf

echo ""
echo "=== Did it survive? ==="
if grep -q "Generated by dhcpcd" /etc/resolv.conf; then
  echo "FAIL: dhcpcd rewrote resolv.conf - nohook not working"
else
  echo "PASS: resolv.conf untouched by dhcpcd rebind"
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Network still healthy ==="
ping -c 1 -W 2 192.168.0.1 >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "gateway OK" || echo "gateway FAIL"
getent hosts github.com >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "DNS OK" || echo "DNS FAIL"
